rows.mood_summary: The city feels mixed. Right now the pressure is mostly around general, transit, routine. The signal still feels live today.

drivers.excerpt: the only way to stay sane here is to treat every overground delay like a personal insult you are too tired to process properly
victoria line delay hit at the exact point in the morning where everyone has already decided not to be dramatic about it
my theory is that you can tell how long someone has lived here by how little they react when the tube says severe delays
the weird thing about london is people will apologise for touching your sleeve on the overground and then send you a rent increase like it's weather
Night bus confessions. Bromley. Caught a late 61 last night, driver had the best playlist. It’s weird how the city feels more alive at 3 AM than any other time.

drivers.excerpt: ringbahn wieder chaos und trotzdem stehen alle da als wäre das nur eine weitere Wetterlage. berlin macht aus allem ein Klima
ringbahn delay this morning and one entire carriage somehow looked dressed for three different emotional climates at once
nothing says berlin rent discourse like a new cafe where the stools look temporary but the prices sound permanent
the best version of sunday closure is when the whole city goes quiet enough that the spati fridge suddenly sounds philosophical

taqueria. Mission. The line looks like a small housing crisis but moves fast. Best move is to go when the fog is still deciding what kind of day it is.
